children.name: Sylvia
children.description: Scrub warblers of hedgerows, thickets, and forest edge. The Blackcap is one of Sweden's finest songsters, a rich outpouring of clear fluted notes; the Common Whitethroat perches conspicuously on bramble tops to deliver its scratchy, cheerful song.

children.name: Sylvia atricapilla
children.description: One of Sweden's finest songsters, the male's rich fluty warble pouring from woodland and gardens, the sexes told apart by black (male) or chestnut (female) caps. Increasing numbers now winter in Britain rather than the Mediterranean.

children.name: Sylvia borin
children.description: A plain, featureless brown warbler whose anonymity is offset by a sustained, mellow, babbling song from dense scrub and young woodland. A long-distance migrant wintering in sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Sylvia communis
children.description: A restless warbler of brambly hedgerows and field margins, the male delivering a scratchy song from an exposed perch or in a brief dancing song-flight. It winters in the African Sahel.

children.name: Sylvia curruca
children.description: A small, neat warbler with a dark mask and a distinctive song that ends in a dry rattle, skulking in tall hedges and thickets. It migrates south-east towards north-east Africa via the eastern Mediterranean.

children.name: Sylvia nisoria
children.description: A large, heavy warbler with a fierce yellow eye and barred underparts in the adult, often nesting near Red-backed Shrikes. It reaches the western edge of its range in south-east Sweden.

children.name: Sylvia melanocephala
children.description: A common resident warbler of Mediterranean scrub, the male with a glossy black cap, red eye-ring, and white throat; one of the most widespread breeders of southern Europe.

children.name: Sylvia undata
children.description: A small, dark, long-tailed warbler of lowland heath and Mediterranean scrub; northern populations crash in hard winters. Near Threatened in parts of its range.

children.name: Sylvia cantillans
children.description: A slim Mediterranean warbler, the male with a brick-red throat bordered by a white moustache, breeding in open woodland and scrub.

children.name: Sylvia hortensis
children.description: A large, pale-eyed warbler of open Mediterranean woodland and olive groves, with a black-capped male and rich fluty song.

children.name: Sylvia rueppelli
children.description: A scrub warbler of the eastern Mediterranean, the male with a black throat and crown separated by a white moustachial stripe.

children.name: Sylvia melanothorax
children.description: A scrub warbler endemic as a breeder to Cyprus, the male heavily spotted black below; winters in the eastern Mediterranean and Middle East.

children.name: Sylvia sarda
children.description: A grey, long-tailed Mediterranean scrub warbler breeding on Corsica, Sardinia, and nearby islands.

children.name: Sylvia conspicillata
children.description: A small, rufous-winged warbler of low coastal scrub and saltmarsh around the Mediterranean and Macaronesia.

children.name: Sylvia deserticola
children.description: A warbler of North African mountain scrub and Atlas woodland, the male warm rufous below.

children.name: Sylvia leucomelaena
children.description: A black-capped warbler of acacia scrub in the southern Levant, Arabia, and northeast Africa, often cocking and dipping its tail.

children.name: Sylvia mystacea
children.description: A scrub warbler of the Caucasus, Middle East, and Central Asia, the male with a pinkish flush and dark cap; winters in northeast Africa.

children.name: Sylvia balearica
children.description: A grey scrub warbler endemic to the Balearic Islands, closely related to Marmora's Warbler.

children.name: Sylvia crassirostris
children.description: A large pale-eyed warbler of the eastern Mediterranean and Middle East, wintering in northeast Africa and Arabia.

children.name: Sylvia subalpina
children.description: A Mediterranean warbler split from the Subalpine Warbler, breeding on Corsica, Sardinia, and northwest Italy.

children.name: Sylvia althaea
children.description: A whitethroat of Central Asian mountains and foothills, wintering in the Indian subcontinent. Its song is a sweet, sustained warbling phrase.

children.name: Sylvia minula
children.description: A small, pale whitethroat of Central Asian desert scrub and semi-desert, with a sandy-grey back and white throat. It winters in the Middle East and northeast Africa.

children.name: Sylvia margelanica
children.description: A whitethroat of northwestern China and adjacent Central Asia, sometimes treated as a subspecies of the Lesser Whitethroat. It has paler upperparts and a shorter tail.

children.name: Sylvia nana
children.description: A small, pale sandy-coloured warbler of arid scrub and semi-desert from the Caspian Sea to Central Asia. It constantly flicks its wings while foraging.

children.name: Sylvia deserti
children.description: A pale, sandy warbler of the Saharan and Sahelian desert fringes, with a reddish tail and pale yellow eye. It is a resident of the driest regions of North Africa.

children.name: Sylvia buryi
children.description: A dark warbler endemic to the highlands of southwestern Arabia, found in juniper and acacia woodlands. Its song is a loud, scratchy warble.

children.name: Sylvia lugens
children.description: A grey-brown warbler of the Ethiopian highlands and East African mountains, with a rufous-brown tail and pale throat.

children.name: Sylvia subcoerulea
children.description: A warbler of southern African savanna and thornveld, grey above and white below with a distinctive rufous vent and undertail coverts.

children.name: Sylvia dohrni
children.description: A warbler endemic to the island of Príncipe and São Tomé in the Gulf of Guinea, with a white throat, grey belly, and olive-brown back. It inhabits primary forest understorey.

children.name: Sylvia galinieri
children.description: A warbler of the Ethiopian highlands, with a white throat and eyestripe, grey upperparts, and warm buff underparts above 2000 m.

children.name: Sylvia nigricapillus
children.description: A round-headed, short-tailed warbler of South African montane forests, the male with a glossy black cap contrasting with a bright yellow-orange eye.

children.name: Sylvia abyssinica
children.description: A skulking warbler-like bird of African montane forest understorey.

children.name: Sylvia boehmi
children.description: A small grey warbler of dry acacia bush in East Africa, marked with a narrow black breast band.

children.name: Sylvia layardi
children.description: A grey, white-eyed warbler of arid karoo scrub in southern Africa.

children.name: Sylvia atriceps
children.description: A dark-headed warbler of the Albertine Rift mountains, with a black cap, olive back, and yellow underparts.

children.name: Rhopophilus
children.description: The Chinese hill warblers — long-tailed, skulking birds of northern Chinese scrub and hillsides, with a complex, musical song. They were long considered babblers but molecular studies place them firmly within the sylviid radiation.

children.name: Rhopophilus pekinensis
children.description: A long-tailed, brown-and-white warbler of scrubby hillsides in northern and central China, with a bubbling, melodic song. It skulks in dense vegetation but sings from exposed perches.

children.name: Rhopophilus albosuperciliaris
children.description: A recently split species from the Chinese Hill Warbler, with a bolder white supercilium and paler underparts. It is restricted to the mountains around Beijing and neighbouring provinces.

children.name: Chrysomma
children.description: Yellow-eyed babblers — small, warm-brown birds of tall grasslands and reeds in South and Southeast Asia, with bright orange-yellow irises and a habit of creeping through dense stems like mice.

children.name: Chrysomma sinense
children.description: A warm rufous-brown bird with a long tapering tail, white throat, and brilliant orange-yellow eye. It moves in small parties through rank grass and scrub across the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Chrysomma altirostre
children.description: A small, brown babbler of reedbeds and tall grasslands in the Indus Valley, Myanmar, and Java. It is secretive and easily overlooked, often detected by its thin, high-pitched calls. IUCN status: Vulnerable.

children.name: Fulvetta
children.description: Fulvettas — small, brownish babblers of Asian montane forests, formerly placed with the laughingthrushes but now recognised as sylviid warblers. They are active, gregarious foragers in the understorey and bamboo thickets.

children.name: Fulvetta cinereiceps
children.description: A small, brown fulvetta of eastern Chinese montane forests, with a grey head, prominent white eye-ring, and fine dark streaking on the throat and breast.

children.name: Fulvetta formosana
children.description: A fulvetta endemic to the mountains of Taiwan, with a warm brown body, grey crown, and prominent buff wing-bar. It forages actively in mixed-species flocks in montane forest.

children.name: Fulvetta danisi
children.description: A grey-headed fulvetta of upland forests in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia, with a white eye-ring, rufous-brown back, and pale underparts.

children.name: Fulvetta manipurensis
children.description: A small, brown fulvetta of the eastern Himalayan foothills and adjacent ranges, with a grey head, white throat, and rufous-brown wings.

children.name: Fulvetta striaticollis
children.description: A fulvetta of central Chinese mountain forests, with a grey head, bold white eye-ring, and streaked throat and breast.

children.name: Fulvetta vinipectus
children.description: A small, active fulvetta of Himalayan and Chinese montane forests, with a white brow, dark eyestripe, and buff underparts. It is a lively and conspicuous member of mixed-species flocks.

children.name: Fulvetta ruficapilla
children.description: A fulvetta of central Chinese forests with a distinctive rufous cap and white eye-ring, giving a spectacled appearance. It inhabits mountain broadleaf and mixed forests.

children.name: Fulvetta ludlowi
children.description: A little-known fulvetta of the eastern Himalayas, closely related to the White-browed Fulvetta but darker and with a more restricted range in northeastern India and Bhutan.

children.name: Fulvetta claudi
children.description: A recently described fulvetta from the mountains of Yunnan in southwestern China, where it inhabits bamboo thickets in montane forest. It is grey-headed with warm brown upperparts.

children.name: Fulvetta sordidior
children.description: A dull-plumaged fulvetta of the eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asian highlands, with a grey head, brown back, and darker throat.

children.name: Fulvetta przewalskii
children.description: A fulvetta from the mountains of western China, with a distinctive white throat and warm brown body. It is poorly known and occupies a restricted range.

children.name: Lioparus
children.description: A monotypic genus for the Golden-breasted Fulvetta — a dazzling little bird of Asian montane bamboo forests with a brilliant golden-yellow breast and a complex, bubbling song.

children.name: Lioparus chrysotis
children.description: A stunning small bird with a bright golden-yellow throat and breast, grey cap, and black-and-white wing pattern. It moves through bamboo thickets and forest understorey in small flocks across the Himalayas and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Chleuasicus
children.description: Small, stout parrotbills of Asian bamboo and grasslands, with thick finch-like bills adapted for splitting bamboo and grass stems to extract insects and seeds.

children.name: Chleuasicus atrosuperciliaris
children.description: A delicate, long-tailed parrotbill with a prominent black brow and soft buff body, moving in family groups through bamboo and rich understorey in the eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Conostoma
children.description: A monotypic genus for the Great Parrotbill — the largest of all the parrotbills, with a massive, conical yellow bill and a robust, thrush-like body. It inhabits high-altitude bamboo forests.

children.name: Conostoma aemodium
children.description: The largest parrotbill, with a huge yellow cone-shaped bill, a grey body, and a long tail. Found in bamboo and rhododendron thickets of the Himalayas and western China above 2500 m.

children.name: Moupinia
children.description: A monotypic genus for the Rufous-tailed Babbler, a long-tailed, skulking bird of scrub and thickets in central China with a rich rufous tail and melodious song.

children.name: Moupinia poecilotis
children.description: A warm brown bird with a striking rufous tail, white throat streaked with black, and a pale eye. It skulks in dense scrub on arid hillsides of central China, often flicking its tail.

children.name: Myzornis
children.description: A monotypic genus for the Fire-tailed Myzornis — one of the most spectacularly coloured birds in Asia, with vivid green, blue, yellow, black, and red plumage. It forages in mixed-species flocks in the mossy canopy of Himalayan cloud forests.

children.name: Myzornis pyrrhoura
children.description: A jewel-like bird with emerald-green body, black face mask, yellow throat, and brilliant red patches on the wing and tail. It feeds on nectar and insects in high-elevation forests of the Himalayas and western China.
